ready.body: Press the button below when you're ready. No rush.

finalized.fallbackText: Next time, push the inference chain one step further.
finalized.note: This goes into your training file. Next time you run the five rounds, the AI puts it in the first line.

q1.title: When you captured this signal, where was it in the narrative arc?

options.pre_narrative: Silent phase · no one outside the circle is talking; a physical variable, not noise, set me off
options.early: Early leading · insiders are murmuring, mainstream media hasn't covered it
options.mid: Mid spread · sell-side is publishing, but the disagreement is still live
options.late: Late consensus · headlines and retail are all on it; I captured it late

q1.openPrompt: What's your basis for placing it in this phase? (name one or two specific signals, not "a feeling")

q2.title: How many hops did your inference chain run? Which lenses did you use?

options.first_one_lens: First-order · one lens · who obviously benefits (stayed on the surface, the latticework never opened)
options.second_one_lens: Second-order · one lens · who's forced to concede (there's a second order, but only finance or business)
options.third_multi_lens: Third-order · multi-lens · cross-disciplinary (law / engineering / game theory / history) surfaced a contrarian winner
options.wavered: Talked myself back partway — the chain ran, but I overrode it

q2.openPrompt: If you ran it again, which lens did you miss? (pick one: psychology / law / history / game theory / biology / engineering / chemistry / physics / math)

q3.title: Can your exit conditions turn this position's convexity back into cash?

options.specific_anchored: Yes · all three anchors: a price anchor + a time anchor + one externally observable signal
options.price_only: Half · only a price stop, no intrinsic margin of safety — one drop and it's empty
options.vague: Empty · what I wrote amounts to "it depends"; the convexity can't be realized
options.wrong_kind: Wrong type · this position never should've had exit conditions (it's really a hedge / pair / long-term own)

q3.openPrompt: Next time you write exit conditions, what will your three anchors be? (price + time + external)

q4.title: From signing to acting, where did your hesitation cost come from?

options.same_day: Same day · acted immediately, no hesitation
options.within_week: Within a week · acted after one pass of base-rate calibration
options.weeks_later: Dragged for weeks · the inside view's overconfidence had to be knocked down repeatedly first
options.never: Signed but never really acted · thesis and position broke apart

q4.openPrompt: If you hesitated, which lens were you re-checking? (uneasy about the base rate, or judging reflexive timing, or something else)
